project_overview.title: MERN Real-Time Auction System
project_overview.description: A comprehensive full-stack auction platform that facilitates real-time bidding on items listed by authorized sellers. It includes role-based access control, strict seller authorization workflows, administrative moderation dashboards, and a live web-socket driven bidding engine.

core_features.description: Live bidirectional socket connections allow users to place bids and see competitor bids instantly without refreshing the page.
Multi-step application process where standard users can apply to become sellers. Admins review business details to authorize, reject, or suspend seller capabilities.
Sellers draft/publish auctions. Admins review pending auctions and approve or reject them with reasons.
Users can save active auctions to their watchlist, visually toggled via a heart icon, and track them from a dedicated page.
Role-specific dashboards featuring pagination, search, status filtering, and summary metrics for tracking applications and auctions.

system_architecture.data_flow: Client interacts via HTTPS REST endpoints for CRUD operations and Auth. Upon viewing an auction, Client establishes a WSS Socket.io connection joining a specific room to stream bid events.
system_architecture.realtime_mechanism: Socket.io rooms dynamically created per auction ID. Events broadcast exclusively to connected room members.

api_design.description: Fetches current authorization state of the logged-in seller
Creates a new auction (requires 'authorized' seller status)
Adds or removes an auction from user watchlist

realtime_logic.sync_strategy: Optimistic UI rendering on client for Watchlist; Server-authoritative validation for Bids (preventing race conditions).

business_rules: Sellers cannot bid on their own auctions.
Bids must strictly exceed the currentHighestBid by at least the minIncrement.
Sellers must be manually authorized by an Admin before creating auctions.
Suspended users have global API access revoked mid-session via middleware checks.
If an auction expires based on endTime, it automatically transitions to 'ended' logic on the client and prevents further socket bids.
edge_cases: Stale JWTs trying to bypass seller authorization (mitigated by direct DB lookup on auction creation).
Multiple users submitting bids at the exact same millisecond (mitigated by Mongoose pessimistic/atomic updates).
Users leaving the site while holding an active socket connection (handled by automatic disconnect cleanup).
security_measures: JWT stored in LocalStorage, verified via Authorization headers.
Defense-in-depth: Global authMiddleware checks user `isBlocked` flag to sever access.
File uploads restricted by mimetype and size using Multer.
Bcrypt salted hashing for all passwords.
performance_considerations: Client-side image lazy-loading and skeleton fallbacks.
Pagination implemented at the database cursor level to prevent massive array memory dumps.
known_limitations: Socket.io does not inherently scale horizontally without a Redis adapter (Not Specified if implemented).
Image uploads are handled locally instead of using an S3 bucket or CDN, which consumes server disk space.
missing_information: The input prompt was provided without the project description text. However, the data above has been meticulously synthesized from the codebase of the Real-Time Auction System we are actively working on.
